meaning: n.[船]特等客舱,[火车]高级包厢,政府公寓
examples: He fled to his stateroom, where he lurked until the steamer was clear of the dock.
But what good was sitting alone in my stateroom?
"You had better slip down into my stateroom now" I said moving off stealthily.
This will allow you to make calls from your stateroom, on a direct dial basis, for two dollars a minute.
Carroll's boats are crammed with electronic gear, from satellite phone systems to stereos that can be controlled from each stateroom.
